As the sit-in by anti-corruption campaigners grows in New Delhi, opposition lawmakers relay their call for the education minister to resign over the leaking of exam tests. items.title: Sex trafficking in fashion: 'Outrageous that French legal system hasn't done anything by now'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Dana Thomas, Paris-based journalist, specialising in fashion and culture, and author of "Deluxe: How Luxury Lost Its Luster". For decades, allegations of sexual exploitation in the fashion industry, spanning the globe, have resurfaced, often surrounding powerful figures whose deaths have complicated any semblance of accountability. In the wake of Daniel Siad's sudden death, Thomas offers a scathing indictment of the systemic failures within French law enforcement, the modelling industry, and the highest echelons of power.

items.description: Mali’s military has suffered one of its deadliest losses in years. At least 50 soldiers were killed in an ambush near the northern town of Anefis, in an attack claimed by Tuareg separatists and Al-Qaeda-linked jihadists. The assault is the latest in a string of setbacks, with militants also targeting Malian and Russian Africa Corps forces, downing a military helicopter, and briefly seizing territory. items.title: Your guide to every day of the Commonwealth Games in Australian time
items.description: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

items.title: New Bundesliga TV deal in US prioritizes reach over money
items.description: The Bundesliga has a new home on American screens after Germany's top football league left ESPN. The new deal is a sign of the times.

items.title: 40th edition of the Nuits d'Afrique music festival comes to a close
items.description: Forty years after its first edition, Montreal’s Nuits d’Afrique international music festival once again celebrated the influence of African cultures.

items.title: France's D-Day landing beaches could become a UNESCO World Heritage site in July
items.description: Normandy’s application to have the D-Day beaches in northern France listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site could finally be accepted at the end of July, after it's submission nearly 20 years ago. The nomination also includes Pointe du Hoc, the German artillery battery at Longues-sur-Mer, Port Mulberry, and numerous military cemeteries and memorials. The UN cultural agency is set to male a decision between July 24 and 27.

items.title: Liberia seizes record cocaine haul near Monrovia, officials say
items.description: Liberian police say they have made the country's biggest drug bust ever, after raiding a suspected storage site near the capital and finding what officials believe is a huge quantity of cocaine.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will destroy bridge or power plant for each Iranian attack in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The U.S. carried out another wave of strikes on Iran overnight into Wednesday, and air defences opened fire over the capital, Tehran.

items.title: Ukraine hits more warehouses of top Russian retailer Wildberries
items.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv struck "logistics hubs" supplying the Russian army with "drone components, navigation equipment and other gear".

items.title: Inside CPAC London: Charlatans, conspiracy theories and loud snoring
items.description: In 10 years at the ABC, I've had some strange experiences. I can now add last week's CPAC conference in London to the list, and it's going somewhere near the top, says Riley Stuart in London.

items.title: Trump push for Syrian intervention against Hezbollah ‘short-sighted’
items.description: Analysts say US president's pressure on Damascus to intervene in Lebanon could undermine domestic security.

items.title: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' storms the box office
items.description: Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey” launched with an estimated $124.5 million in US ticket sales and another $139.6 million from overseas, notching an even better debut than “Oppenheimer” and marking the filmmaker’s best opening since 2012’s “The Dark Knight Rises.”

items.title: 'It's all literally rubble': Lebanese poet returns to see what has become of her home
items.description: A village destroyed. A home erased. One poet returns to remember what was lost.

items.title: 'Cockroach Janta Party' takes India by storm
items.description: Born from a satirical retort to a passing comment by India's top judge, the "Cockroach Janta Party" has grown into a full-fledged movement protesting the government's mishandling of the nationwide medical entrance examination in May. Despite a violent crackdown on a peaceful march heading to the Indian parliament in New Delhi on Monday, members of the movement have kept up their protest. FRANCE 24 explains how the CJP came to be.

items.title: British woman jailed for blackmail after accusing banker of rape in Hong Kong
items.description: Isabel Rose was convicted of trying to extort £100,000 from the UK banker and perverting the course of justice.

items.title: Senegal backs Macky Sall's bid for UN Secretary-General
items.description: Senegal has officially thrown its support behind former President Macky Sall's bid to become the next United Nations Secretary-General, marking a dramatic policy shift.

items.description: An Israeli strike hit a civilian vehicle in central Gaza, killing two Palestinians.

items.title: New York mayor says city can't arrest Netanyahu on ICC warrant
items.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani said Tuesday his office does not have the authority to carry out the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, and called on US federal officials to step in

items.title: Six jailed Ivory Coast opposition party officials freed after months in detention
items.description: The Abidjan PDCI officials, arrested in October last year, were released on Monday after nine months in detention.

items.title: Is Germany investing in the right weapons?
items.description: Germany wants to build up its army and pour hundreds of millions of euros into its defenses. But in a world where the battlefield is increasingly dominated by cheap drones, how many expensive tanks does the country need?
